routes.title: Resolve one exact Clay alternative
routes.question: Does every admissible smooth flow remain globally smooth, or can admissible smooth data force breakdown?
routes.success: A complete proof of A, B, C, or D.
routes.kill_criterion: Only a terminal Clay-matching theorem closes this root.

routes.title: Global regularity
routes.question: Can every putative concentration be prevented or reduced to an impossible object?
routes.success: Global smoothness on R3 or T3.
routes.kill_criterion: A Clay-admissible smooth-data singularity would kill this universal route.

routes.title: Universal critical control
routes.question: Can the equation itself bound or shrink a critical continuation quantity?
entry_assumptions: Smooth solution on its maximal interval
routes.success: A universal critical bound feeding an established continuation criterion.
routes.kill_criterion: An exact smooth evolution with unbounded candidate quantity but no alternate criterion.

routes.title: Velocity endpoint
routes.question: Can velocity be kept in L-infinity time L3 space or a stronger critical class?
routes.success: Derive the ESS or finite-q Lorentz hypothesis from Navier-Stokes.
routes.kill_criterion: Prove that the chosen endpoint can diverge in a smooth maximal solution without yielding another bound.

routes.title: Vorticity-direction depletion
routes.question: Does viscous dynamics force enough alignment or vanishing mean oscillation near intense vorticity?
routes.success: A universal geometric modulus that closes all high-vorticity cores.
routes.kill_criterion: Construct an admissible concentrating flow geometry violating every proposed modulus.

routes.title: Frequency-flux barrier
routes.question: Does the exact triadic structure prevent an indefinitely accelerating cascade?
entry_assumptions: The true projected Navier-Stokes bilinear operator
routes.success: A scale-local or nonlocal flux estimate yielding subcritical decay.
routes.kill_criterion: A certified true-nonlinearity cascade that crosses infinitely many scales in finite time.

routes.title: Analyticity versus sparseness
routes.question: Must dangerous superlevel sets become sparse inside the viscous analytic radius?
routes.success: A universal sparseness criterion at every possible escape time.
routes.kill_criterion: A surviving concentration whose sparse scale never enters the analytic radius.

routes.title: Minimal blow-up and rigidity
routes.question: Can failure be reduced to a compact ancient object and that object be ruled out?
routes.success: A reduction theorem plus a Liouville or backward-uniqueness contradiction.
routes.kill_criterion: Essential noncompactness that cannot be captured by the selected critical topology.

routes.title: Concentration compactness
routes.question: Can translations, dilations, and frequency drift be normalized into a compact minimal solution?
entry_assumptions: A finite critical threshold or minimality principle
routes.success: Precompactness modulo exact Navier-Stokes symmetries.
routes.kill_criterion: A rigorously demonstrated multi-profile cascade with no minimal compact representative.

routes.title: Ancient-solution classification
routes.question: Which bounded or critical ancient solutions can exist?
entry_assumptions: An ancient limit with inherited local energy and critical bounds
routes.success: Classification reduces the limit to zero or an already excluded profile.
routes.kill_criterion: Construction of a nontrivial ancient solution meeting every inherited bound.

routes.title: Backward uniqueness and Liouville rigidity
routes.question: Can vanishing at a terminal slice force the ancient object to vanish identically?
entry_assumptions: Enough decay and coefficient control for unique continuation
routes.success: A contradiction with nontrivial normalized concentration.
routes.kill_criterion: A counterexample at the exact regularity and growth level inherited from rescaling.

routes.title: Remove assumptions from conditional criteria
routes.question: Can a structured criterion be extended until it covers every singularity class?
routes.success: A finite covering/reduction whose leaves are all excluded.
routes.kill_criterion: An explicit survivor outside all derived criteria.

routes.title: Single point to arbitrary cores
routes.question: Can one replace a fixed ball by moving, multiple, or anisotropic coverings without losing the gain?
entry_assumptions: Geometry beyond bare weak-L(3/2) volume control
routes.success: A covering theorem whose entropy cost remains absorbable.
routes.kill_criterion: A packing geometry whose necessary covering loss consumes the entire subcritical gain.

routes.title: Imposed geometry to derived geometry
routes.question: Can the nonzero coherent weak-L3 ancient outer profile forced by centering escape, which is not continuously backward self-similar, whose terminal trace has a recursive positive Besov critical-blow-down defect, and which occurs on the same physical trajectory at punctured intermediate radii, be forced into another rigidity class, assigned a forbidden cross-scale flux, or recoupled to the Type-I core still lost at spatial infinity; can the bounded-centering singular-point clock packet be coupled to the subnatural tensor carrier; can the other finite-horizon or eternal parent candidates be excluded; and, separately, can any stronger carrier-to-next-parent genealogy be constructed for a two-edge suitable-process argument?
entry_assumptions: Smoothness before a first singular time
routes.success: A scale-recurrence, same-trajectory flux, or multiscale law forces the evolved outer profile into an excluded continuous or discrete self-similar class, forbids its recursive terminal Besov defect, recovers the escaped Type-I core, or forces bounded centering; a tensor and frequency bridge then excludes the remaining parent-local concentration, and a first-singular-time history bound or rigidity theorem excludes the other finite-horizon and eternal fixed-shell parent candidates and forces zero direction-weighted trace concentration; a separate nested-array theorem is required only for any two-edge shift-process route.
routes.kill_criterion: A Clay-admissible single trajectory or nonzero discrete, asymptotically recurrent, or scale-aperiodic coherent weak-L3 ancient suitable outer-profile tower with recursive terminal Besov defects at arbitrarily deep punctured physical scales and its logarithmically divergent isolated Type-I core permanently lost at spatial infinity; or a parent-local finer continuation concentration that sheds every tensor mark, an infinite nested tower of fixed-mass subnatural tensor-oscillation microbubbles, constant coarser-scale jet decorations, surviving multiscale stress and positive projected Young moments, and tensor defect outside every available rigidity class.

routes.title: Critical profile to Type-II dynamics
routes.question: Can the criterion survive rates and geometries not locked to point-critical scaling?
routes.success: A scale-adaptive estimate covering Type-II and oscillatory concentration.
routes.kill_criterion: A coherent Type-II mechanism outside the scale-adaptive estimate.

routes.title: Finite-time breakdown
routes.question: Can a singular mechanism be constructed with exact Clay-admissible smooth data?
entry_assumptions: Choose alternative C or D, or disprove A or B by smooth-data blow-up
routes.success: No global classical solution for explicitly admissible data.
routes.kill_criterion: A proof of global regularity closes this side.

routes.title: Unforced smooth-data singularity
routes.question: Can the true nonlinearity drive a positive-time singularity from smooth data?
entry_assumptions: Smooth rapidly decaying or periodic divergence-free data
routes.success: A rigorous finite-time continuation failure.
routes.kill_criterion: A proof of Clay A or B.

routes.title: Similarity mechanism
routes.question: Can a non-excluded backward, discrete, asymptotic, or Type-II similarity profile be certified?
routes.success: An exact profile plus stability and smooth-prehistory matching.
routes.kill_criterion: A classification theorem excluding the entire selected similarity class.

routes.title: Non-self-similar cascade
routes.question: Can true triadic interactions transfer activity through infinitely many scales in finite time?
routes.success: A certified cascade with a classical smooth prehistory.
routes.kill_criterion: A universal flux or continuation bound.

routes.title: Smooth-forced Clay construction
routes.question: Can allowed smooth forcing organize a breakdown while satisfying Fefferman's exact conditions?
entry_assumptions: Alternative C or D force class
routes.success: An exact force and proof of no global smooth solution.
routes.kill_criterion: Global regularity for every force in the official class.

routes.title: Bridge singular-data mechanisms to Clay data
routes.question: Can certified rough-threshold instability be reached from a smooth earlier state?
entry_assumptions: An exact unstable forward self-similar profile
routes.success: Smooth-prehistory matching or an admissible smooth-forcing embedding.
routes.kill_criterion: A no-entry theorem showing smooth strong branches cannot reach the unstable manifold.

routes.title: Independent certified profile reproduction
routes.question: Do the profile, residual, spectral, and fixed-point certificates reproduce independently?
routes.success: Independent checkable certificates for the claimed exact objects.
routes.kill_criterion: A nonrepairable mismatch in a theorem-critical certificate.

routes.title: Smooth prehistory or force embedding
routes.question: Can the certified unstable manifold be entered without singular initial data?
routes.success: Exact positive-time matching from smooth data or an official smooth force.
routes.kill_criterion: A rigorous invariant barrier or weak-strong uniqueness exclusion.
